title: Habit Formation
puzzle: Why do repeated behaviors start to feel automatic?
insight: Habits form when the brain learns to link cues, actions, and outcomes into repeatable patterns that require less conscious effort.
key_ideas: Cues often trigger habits before you notice them.
Changing context can help disrupt old patterns.
mind_blower: A habit is a decision your brain is trying to make in advance so you do not spend energy deciding each time.
next_curiosity: If habits reduce effort, how can people use that to build better routines?
narration_text: Why do repeated behaviors start to feel automatic? Habits form when the brain learns to link cues, actions, and outcomes into repeatable patterns that require less conscious effort. Repetition strengthens behavioral loops. Cues often trigger habits before you notice them. Changing context can help disrupt old patterns. A habit is a decision your brain is trying to make in advance so you do not spend energy deciding each time. If habits reduce effort, how can people use that to build better routines?
